instructions: From Search Console: Performance → Queries (impressions >20, clicks ~0, position 10–30). Open each URL; set slug to the segment after /blog/ (single segment, no leading slash), enabled to true. From nursenest-core: npx tsx scripts/blog-apply-gsc-opportunity-upgrades.mts (dry-run by default, no DB writes). After review: npx tsx scripts/blog-apply-gsc-opportunity-upgrades.mts --apply

upgrades.title: Hyperkalemia Effects on the Heart (NCLEX Mechanism + ECG Changes)
upgrades.seoTitle: Hyperkalemia Effects on the Heart (NCLEX Mechanism + ECG Changes)
upgrades.seoDescription: How high potassium changes cardiac resting potential, slows conduction, and produces peaked T waves → wide QRS → sine wave. Exam-focused pathophysiology plus where to practice.
upgrades.excerpt: High potassium shifts cardiac resting membrane potential and inactivates sodium channels—peaked T waves first, then conduction delays. Learn the mechanism NCLEX ties to priority actions.
upgrades.bodyLeadHtml:
Answer in one beat: hyperkalemia raises extracellular potassium, making the resting membrane potential less negative (partial depolarization). Myocytes become irritable early, then sodium-channel inactivation slows phase 0—so conduction delays. On ECG expect peaked T waves first, then PR prolongation, QRS widening, sine waves, and lethal arrhythmias without treatment. That is the physiology the heart “shows” on the monitor and the stem wants you to link to immediate stabilization and treatment.

Reinforce actions and monitoring in the fluids & electrolyte emergencies lesson and question bank.
upgrades.bodyClusterHtml:
Cluster: potassium & the ECG

  • Hypo- vs hyperkalemia / emergencies (lesson)
  • NCLEX-RN lesson hub
  • Question bank

queriesTargeted: copd barrel chest air trapping mechanism
upgrades.title: COPD Barrel Chest & Air Trapping (Mechanism for NCLEX)
upgrades.seoTitle: COPD Barrel Chest & Air Trapping (Mechanism for NCLEX)
upgrades.seoDescription: Why COPD can show a barrel-shaped thorax: loss of elastic recoil, dynamic airway collapse on exhale, hyperinflation—and how exam items test it.
upgrades.excerpt: Barrel chest in COPD reflects chronic hyperinflation and air trapping: destroyed alveolar support lets small airways collapse on exhale while gas stays distal—AP diameter increases.
upgrades.targetKeyword: copd barrel chest air trapping mechanism
upgrades.bodyLeadHtml:
Answer in one breath: in emphysema-heavy COPD, loss of alveolar elastic recoil removes radial traction on small airways. During exhalation, those airways narrow or collapse while distal gas is trapped (air trapping). The chest chronically sits nearer total lung capacity—ribs ride more horizontal and anteroposterior diameter increases, which is the classic barrel chest silhouette. It signals chronic hyperinflation, not by itself a new acute event.

Apply GOLD-style priorities and O₂ teaching in the COPD management lesson and drill scenarios in the question bank.
